name: SuperPawn
description_short: FirstCash is the leading international operator of pawn stores with 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states, the UK, and Latin America, offering pawn loans secured by personal property.
description_long:
FirstCash, Inc. is headquartered in Fort Worth, Texas and operates as the largest international pawn store chain. Founded on the principle of serving cash and credit-constrained consumers, the company has built a network exceeding 3,300 retail locations across 29 U.S. states, Washington D.C., the United Kingdom, and throughout Latin America including Mexico, Guatemala, Colombia, and El Salvador. The company employs approximately 22,000 people and is publicly traded as a component of both the S&P MidCap 400 Index and Russell 2000 Index.

FirstCash's core business model centers on retail pawn lending and merchandise sales. Customers can obtain non-recourse pawn loans secured by pledged personal property such as jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, and musical instruments. Beyond pawn loans, FirstCash operates retail sales of merchandise, gold/precious metals buying, and layaway services with 10% down payment options. The company's subsidiary AFF provides lease-to-own and retail finance solutions through over 15,000 merchant partner locations nationwide.

FirstCash distinguishes itself through its massive scale as the international market leader in pawn operations and its diversified service offerings beyond basic pawn loans. The company's extensive geographic footprint—particularly its Latin American presence across multiple countries—sets it apart from regional competitors. Their integration of point-of-sale payment solutions and lease-to-own services through AFF creates a broader consumer finance ecosystem beyond traditional pawn operations.

As a pawn lender, FirstCash serves an important role for consumers needing immediate cash without credit checks. However, pawn loans involve asset risk, and the company's scale as a major corporation means less personalization than smaller local pawn shops. Borrowers should understand that failure to repay results in loss of collateral, and while non-recourse loans protect from deficiency judgments, the personal property pledged is forfeited.
pros: 3,300+ locations across 29 U.S. states plus DC, UK, and Latin America provides convenient access for most consumers
No credit check required—non-recourse pawn loans available to credit-constrained consumers
Immediate cash on the spot for items of value without lengthy approval processes
Wide variety of accepted merchandise including jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, and instruments
Layaway service available with affordable 10% down payment option for retail purchases
Gold and precious metals buying service providing spot cash for precious items
Transparent pawn loan process with collateral-based lending model (no hidden credit-based fees)
Publicly traded company with regulatory oversight and established consumer privacy policies
cons: Pawn loans involve forfeiture of personal property if loan cannot be repaid—borrowers lose collateral
Interest rates and terms on pawn loans not disclosed on website; rates vary significantly by location and item
As a large corporation with 3,300+ locations, customer service and negotiation flexibility may be limited compared to independent pawn shops
Retail merchandise quality and inventory not guaranteed; layaway service requires 10% non-refundable down payment
Limited transparency on specific loan terms, redemption periods, and fee structures without visiting a physical location
best_for: Consumers needing immediate cash who own valuable items (jewelry, electronics, tools) and have poor or no credit
People in emergency financial situations requiring same-day funds without credit approval processes
Individuals seeking to buy discounted merchandise, gold, or items from pawn inventory
Customers in rural or underserved areas with convenient access to 3,300+ store locations
diagnosis: FirstCash is best for credit-constrained consumers in immediate need of cash who own valuable personal property and can accept the risk of collateral loss. The main caveat is that pawn loans result in forfeiture of pledged items if repayment occurs—this is not a credit-building solution and should only be used when the item's loss is acceptable or when the borrower is confident in repayment ability.
services: Pawn loans secured by personal property (jewelry, electronics, tools, appliances, sporting goods, musical instruments)
Retail sales of previously pawned and consigned merchandise
Gold, silver, and precious metals buying at spot prices with immediate cash payment
Layaway purchase plans with 10% down payment option
Small non-recourse pawn loans (borrower not liable for deficiency)
Store locator tool for finding nearest location among 3,300+ stores
Pawn FAQ educational resources and how-to guides
Lease-to-own and retail finance solutions through AFF subsidiary partner network
Point-of-sale payment solutions through AFF subsidiary
Gold buying service specifically highlighted as core offering
